Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Category Theory Illustrated – Types 42 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 524 upvotes, 117 comments
  3. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1678 upvotes, 445 comments
  4. A Recipe for Steganogravy 86 upvotes, 16 comments
  5. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 395 upvotes, 123 comments
  6. Show HN: TurboQuant for vector search – 2-4 bit compression 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 62 upvotes, 14 comments
  8. Intel Assured Supply Chain Product Brief 41 upvotes, 8 comments
  9. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 133 upvotes, 42 comments
  10. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 240 upvotes, 94 comments
  11. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 119 upvotes, 44 comments
  12. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 1077 upvotes, 507 comments
  13. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 208 upvotes, 89 comments
  14. If you're running OpenClaw, you probably got hacked in the last week 70 upvotes, 25 comments
  15. Tailscale's new macOS home 529 upvotes, 276 comments
  16. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. Bun: cgroup-aware AvailableParallelism / HardwareConcurrency on Linux 33 upvotes, 12 comments
  18. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 216 upvotes, 121 comments
  19. ESP32-S31: Dual-Core RISC-V SoC with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and Advanced HMI 155 upvotes, 84 comments
  20. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Critics say EU risks ceding control of its tech laws under U.S. pressure 172 upvotes, 104 comments
  23. Cursor 3 509 upvotes, 372 comments
  24. Mercurial Dyson – a plan for the disassembly of planet Mercury 15 upvotes, 5 comments
  25. TDF ejects its core developers 108 upvotes, 77 comments
  26. Understanding young news audiences at a time of rapid change 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 57 upvotes, 38 comments
  28. Marc Andreessen is wrong about introspection 324 upvotes, 316 comments
  29. Pharmaceuticals face 100% tariffs in US – unless firms strike a deal 10 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Solar and batteries can power the world 190 upvotes, 272 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 4 April 2026. Source here.
